title: Cohabitors
description: Cohabitors explores the emotional terrain of shared living. Connection, closeness, conflict, and the quiet isolation that can exist even when we live side by side. Through a performance blending acrobatics, body percussion, and live music, we reflect on the tension between intimacy and disconnection in cramped, high-pressure living situations. The piece was inspired by moving to London for university. Stepping into a fast-paced city where high rent forces people to live in tight spaces. In these conditions, minor issues can grow quickly, and even small interactions carry weight. It asks: What does it really mean to live together? This is Clamber’s first work combining circus, live music, and community-led storytelling. With backgrounds in classical music, circus arts, and digital sound, our artists bring fresh perspectives that challenge convention and invite audiences to engage with circus in a new way.
